mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which triggers immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power during mid-cycle expansions, with rank correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day returns falling below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product confirm sustained economic growth, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data releases.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth fails to precede positive 5-day market returns, with a hit rate below 45% across all quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4% forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises by over 0.4% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when payrolls rise and unemployment falls, with a cumulative alpha below 0.5% per quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in real personal consumption expenditures signal weakening household demand, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails during contractions, with RSAFS declines coinciding with positive 5-day SPX returns in over 55% of cases.
